articles.title: Reggae walk of fame launched in Harlesden
articles.summary: A trail celebrating Harlesden's reggae heritage and Windrush pioneers launches in north-west London.

articles.title: NHS to use AI on its app to direct patients to appropriate services
articles.summary: <p>Update in England expected to reach about 200,000 patients over the next year as part of £10bn package to overhaul NHS systems</p><p>The NHS will begin using AI on its app to direct patients to the appropriate services, it has been announced.</p><p>The tool will be used to triage patients and to ascertain if they should be allocated a GP appointment. Some may be advised to attend a pharmacy or their local A&amp;E department instead, depending on the severity

articles.title: Agentic coding notes from Galapagos Island
Beyoncé releases surprise new track for Fourth of July weekend
articles.summary: <p>Morning Dew (Donk) reportedly conceived for Beyoncé’s second album B’Day, which will be reissued for its 20th anniversary in September</p><p>She popularised the shock drop in 2013 with the surprise release of her self-titled album. And on Saturday, Beyoncé did it again, dropping a new song out of the blue.</p><p>The singer released the new track, titled Morning Dew (Donk), on the Fourth of July holiday weekend. articles.title: Audacious mission to rescue NASA's falling telescope has launched
articles.summary: NASA’s Swift space telescope is reaching the end of its two-decade run in orbit – unless a satellite launched on 3 July can give it a lifesaving boost

articles.title: Beeg float library, a Rust port of Fabrice Bellard's libbf
Zig: All Package Management Functionality Moved from Compiler to Build System
‘Our little adventure’: Angela Merkel unveils portrait of herself in Berlin
articles.summary: <p>Jérémie Queyras, 28, created painting over months of sessions at his studio where pair struck up unlikely bond</p><p>Over months they met secretly in a studio in the government quarter of central Berlin – a young artist and his subject, the former German chancellor Angela Merkel. articles.title: You know the Mayflower. What about the White Lion? Here's the story of 'Two Ships'
articles.summary: David S. Reynolds' book examines the twin legacies of the Mayflower, which carried the Pilgrims to Plymouth in 1620, and the White Lion, which brought the first enslaved Africans to Virginia in 1619.

articles.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
articles.summary: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

articles.title: China releases underground church pastor after months in detention
articles.summary: Ezra Jin’s family says the Zion Church founder arrived in the US after months in detention in China.

articles.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

articles.title: Legally Blonde revival Elle - boring and tedious or a hot pink delight?
articles.summary: The spin-off introduces a new generation to Elle Woods, but critics say young people deserve better.

articles.title: Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
articles.summary: NASA awarded Rocket Lab deals for three dedicated launches using the company's Electron rocket.
